description: You are preparing a cryptography lesson for an after-school coding club. Implement a Caesar cipher in Python using a shift value of 7 that preserves case and leaves non-alphabetic characters unchanged. Use this implementation to encrypt the passage: 'It was all very well to say Drink me but the wise little Alice was not going to do that in a hurry' and then decrypt the result. Save the Python source code to /home/ga/Documents/crypto/caesar_cipher.py, the encrypted ciphertext to /home/ga/Documents/crypto/encrypted.txt, the decrypted text to /home/ga/Documents/crypto/decrypted.txt, and a summary report containing the original plaintext, shift value, and ciphertext to /home/ga/Documents/crypto/report.txt. Create the destination directory if necessary.

metadata.expected_plaintext: It was all very well to say Drink me but the wise little Alice was not going to do that in a hurry
metadata.expected_ciphertext: Pa dhz hss clyf dlss av zhf Kypur tl iba aol dpzl spaasl Hspjl dhz uva nvpun av kv aoha pu h oiyyf
